subtitle: Review recovered user-visible behavior with confidence, gaps, and inference pressure in the open.

sections.title: Capability Map and Highest-Value Recovered Stories
sections.label: Recovered Value
sections.why: Lead with the strongest recovered behavior map before weak areas.

sections.title: Boundary and Failure Coverage
sections.label: Boundary Coverage
sections.why: Show where operator-visible edge behavior is actually evidenced.

sections.title: Coverage Gaps and Evidence Weakness
sections.label: Evidence Gaps
sections.why: Expose missing or weak support around likely user-facing behavior.

sections.title: Low-Confidence Stories and Inferred-vs-Observed Pressure Points
sections.label: Confidence
sections.why: Separate inferred stories from directly observed behavior.

sections.title: Decisions Required for Approval
sections.label: Decision Gate
sections.why: State what the reviewer must accept despite recovery uncertainty.

sections.title: Traceability Map
sections.label: Evidence
sections.why: Tie recovered story claims to exact repository evidence.

sections.title: Validator Status
sections.label: Validation
sections.why: Show validation status for the derived report and approval view.

sections.title: Downstream Impact if Approved
sections.label: Impact
sections.why: Clarify what derived requirements may now treat as stable behavior.

glance_cards.label: Recovered anchors
glance_cards.section_title: Capability Map and Highest-Value Recovered Stories
glance_cards.hint: Strongest recovered capability or story anchors.

glance_cards.label: Evidence gaps
glance_cards.hint: Likely user-facing areas with weak support.

glance_cards.section_title: Low-Confidence Stories and Inferred-vs-Observed Pressure Points
